title: Droperidol (Dorm)
content_markdown:
##### Type
Antipsychotic

- **High-risk medication**

##### Presentations
10mg in 2ml – vial 

##### Actions

- Droperidol is an antipsychotic and a tranquiliser. It gives no analgesia and no amnestic effects

- Acts on several neurotransmitters; antipsychotic actions are thought to be mediated by dopamine blockade

Onset of action: IM 3-10 min 
 Peak action up to 30 min 
 Duration 2-4 hours 

#### Uses
Pharmacological intervention for combative / agitated patients.

#### Adverse Effects

- Arrhythmias, including prolonged QT interval or torsades de pointes (all patients receiving droperidol must have ECG monitoring)

- Extrapyramidal effects (rare)

- Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (rare)

- May lower seizure threshold

#### Contraindications

- Known hypersensitivity

- Parkinson’s disease

- Post ictal

#### Precautions
Patients in severe pain; analgesia should also be provided

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding##### Pregnancy
- Approved for administration in ACTAS setting during trimester one and two, during third trimester only use if alternative management unavailable or ineffective

##### Breastfeeding
- Approved for administration in ACTAS setting, document breastfeeding status in ePCR, caution against breastfeeding in the pre-hospital setting

#### Doses##### Adult (>14 years of age)
- 10mg via IM injection only (no IV/IO use) 
- Repeat once, after 10-15 minutes, if required.

##### Frail / elderly (>65 years)
- 5mg via IM injection only (no IV/IO use) 
- Repeat once, after 10-15 minutes, if required.

##### Paediatric (

Droperidol.text: Reduce dose by half following droperidol use (i.e. up to 1mg/kg IM/IV/IO).
